seoTitle: How to Record Vendor Discounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record vendor discounts in QuickBooks Online so early payment savings and supplier pricing breaks are reflected accurately in your books.
intro: When a supplier offers a discount for early payment or bulk purchasing, recording it correctly keeps your expense totals accurate. This guide explains how to capture vendor discounts in QuickBooks Online without overstating what you actually paid.

steps.heading: Locate the vendor bill
steps.body: Open the bill from the vendor that includes the discount you need to apply.

steps.heading: Apply the discount
steps.body: Enter the discount amount against the bill, either as a line item reduction or through the discount field when making payment.

steps.heading: Choose a discount account
steps.body: Assign the discount to an appropriate income or expense reduction account so it's tracked separately.

steps.heading: Confirm the final payment amount
steps.body: Verify that the amount paid reflects the bill total minus the discount before saving the transaction.

faq.question: Does a vendor discount count as income?
faq.answer: It's usually recorded as a reduction to the related expense rather than as separate income, though your accountant can confirm the best treatment.

faq.question: What if the discount applies to only part of the bill?
faq.answer: You can apply the discount to a specific line item if it only covers part of the purchase rather than the whole bill.

faq.question: Can I set up automatic early payment discounts?
faq.answer: Some discount terms can be tracked manually each time, since QuickBooks Online doesn't always calculate early payment terms automatically.

faq.question: How do vendor discounts affect my reports?
faq.answer: Properly recorded discounts will reduce your expense totals, giving you a more accurate picture of actual costs.
